creator: gabor@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Martin,

I started to take a deeper look on these ports today, since they didn't 
compile in tinderbox and I found out that the problem is that 
www/aolserver doesn't install lang/tcl84-thread even if there's no tcl 
port installed. Please look at the output I got:

[gabor@pluto ~/patches/ports/aolserver]$ pkg_info | grep tcl
[gabor@pluto ~/patches/ports/aolserver]$ make
/usr/local/bin/tclsh8.4: not found
"Makefile", line 45: warning: "echo 'puts [array names tcl_platform 
-exact threaded]' | /usr/local/bin/tclsh8.4" returned non-zero status
===>  aolserver-4.5.0 you need Tcl with threads to use this port, please 
install Tcl from lang/tcl84-thread port and try again.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/home/gabor/patches/ports/aolserver.

You should modify the port to install it if no tcl port is installed and 
only give this hint if a wrong tcl port is installed. Otherwise, the 
installation of this port would be very inconvenient and there would be 
problems with the package building as well, so it definitely can't be 
committed in this form.

And please send a unified diff (diff -Nur olddirectory newdirectory) for 
modifications on existing ports to make our work easier. In this form 
it's easier to see what have been changed. Shar archive is ok for the 
other ones.

Thanks for your effort on working on these ports.

Regards,
Gabor Kovesdan
